response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates a broad polyglot engineering background and genuine high-performance systems experience, the core requirement of deep C++/STL proficiency is almost entirely absent from their recent work — the only C++ exposure on record is a 6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago and a bachelor thesis project, and C++ does not even appear in their listed skills. The role demands current C++ expertise, industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443/SDL), MQTT and industrial protocol experience, and an IoT/industrial automation background, none of which the candidate possesses. Their primary profile is TypeScript, Node.js, and web-oriented full-stack development — a strong profile, but misaligned with this role's core demands.
strengths: High-performance and low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and fibers
Genuine event-driven architecture and distributed systems design through the GISCollective open-source geo-data platform
Node.js and React proficiency directly matches the listed advantageous skills in the job description
concerns: No substantive C++ experience in the past 10+ years; C++ is absent from the skills section and barely surfaces in work history
Zero exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), Secure Development Lifecycle practices, or industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
No industrial automation, IoT, or on-premises industrial platform background relevant to the manufacturing-domain context of this role
